MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants On Lok Senior Health Services, On Lok, Inc., and On Lok Day Services' motion to strike portions of the complaint filed by plaintiff Lisa Pang is granted as to the injunctive relief allegations and denied as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. Injunctive relief is not available because Susan Sook Hing Chang is deceased. (Huntingdon Life Sciences Inc. v. Stop Huntingdon Animal Cruelty USA, Inc. (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 1228, 1266.) Because Ms. Pang has sufficiently pled a cause of action for elder neglect under the Elder and Dependent Adult Act including allegations of fraud by defendants, Ms. Pang has adequately alleged entitlement to punitive damages and enhanced remedies under that Act. While a public health record cannot be used to establish liability nor can it constitute an admission, it does not follow that as a matter of law the public health record lacks any evidentiary value. Defendants' remedy, if any, is to seek an order from the trial judge excluding evidence, not striking an allegation or attachment to the complaint. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants On Lok Senior Health Services, On Lok, Inc., and On Lok Day Services' demurrer to the first cause of action for elder neglect and third cause of action for unfair business practices in the complaint filed by plaintiff Lisa Pang is overruled as to both causes of action. Both causes of action are sufficiently pled. The elder neglect claim passes muster per Fenimore v. Regents of the University of California (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 1339. Much like Fenimore, while the allegations that defendants allowed decedent to fall might be insufficient to support an elder neglect claim, the allegations of a regulatory violation and practices of insufficient staffing, concern with saving money at the expense of the safety of the decedent, self-interested advice and concealment of material information are sufficient to state a cognizable claim under the Elder and Dependent Adult Act. Ms. Pang also sufficiently alleged unfair, unlawful and fraudulent business practices to support a claim under the Unfair Competition Law. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
